construct: Applies digital literacy and computational understanding to interpret problems, create solution-driven approaches, and use Information and Communications Technology (ICT) responsibly and effectively in real-life applications.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Demonstrate the ability to identify and describe ICT components, systems and their functions

subSkills.label: Recognises components and explains input/processing/storage/output working together

elementsOfConstruct.description: Classify and distinguish ICT tools, file types, network structures, and technologies

subSkills.label: Categorises tools, differentiates file formats, compares network topologies/technologies

elementsOfConstruct.description: Develops analytical skills and evaluating ICT systems and problems

subSkills.label: Assesses system behaviour, resolves operational issues, recommends improvements

elementsOfConstruct.description: Evaluate ICT systems, networks and technologies

subSkills.label: Examines performance, compares alternatives, considers sustainability/security/user impact

elementsOfConstruct.description: Applies ethical reasoning in the responsible use of ICT

subSkills.label: Digital rights, privacy, security; responsible and safe technology use

components.structureNotes: SBA Form 2: 9 tasks; Form 3: 7 tasks. Final Exam 5 items/100, 2h30.

exemplars.scenarioTheme: School ICT policy scenario on privacy, cyber safety and responsible use
